[PUBLISHED DATE]: Nov. 11, 2011
[TITLE]: President Barack Obama and First Lady Michelle Obama watch… | Flickr
[METADATA/INTRO]: President Barack Obama and First Lady Michelle Obama watch the retiring of the colors aboard the USS Carl Vinson President Barack Obama and First Lady Michelle Obama watch the retiring of the colors aboard the USS Carl Vinson, docked at...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: President Barack Obama and First Lady Michelle Obama watch the retiring of the colors aboard the USS Carl Vinson, docked at North Island Naval Station in San Diego, Calif., Nov. 11, 2011. University of North Carolina and Michigan State University players paused while sailors on board conducted the ceremony, a nightly tradition carried out at sunset. (Official White House Photo by Pete Souza)

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2011
[TITLE]: For Obama, a Little Basketball Before the Diplomacy - The New York Times
[METADATA/INTRO]: ABOARD THE U.S.S. CARL VINSON – The presidency does have its perks. And for President Obama, basketball’s fan in chief, one was the opportunity on Friday night to watch the No. 1 ranked college basketball team, the University of North...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The teams had long been warming up when Mr. Obama and the first lady, Michelle Obama, arrived as the military band played “Anchors Aweigh.” The president, in khaki pants and a leather Air Force bomber jacket inscribed “Barack Obama, Commander in Chief,” went to center-court for remarks before the game tipoff. 
 “One of the greatest privileges of this job, and one of the greatest responsibilities of this job, is to serve as your commander in chief,” he said. Mr. Obama noted that legislation he supported in Congress would provide incentives for hiring veterans, and he saluted his wife and Dr. Jill Biden, wife of Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr., for their work with military families and a program called Joining Forces that has gotten business commitments for 100,000 jobs for veterans and military spouses.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2011-11-11
[TITLE]: Troops and hoops for Obama, on a Navy warship – San Diego Union-Tribune
[METADATA/INTRO]: CORONADO, Calif.CORONADO, Calif. — Soaking in one of the most spectacular settings of his time in office, President Barack Obama pledged an unwavering commitment to veterans Friday from the deck of an aircraft carrier doubling, for one night, as a...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Once the game started Obama, wearing a bomber jacket, watched from the sidelines, looking thrilled, as Michelle Obama looked on at his side. 
 Despite the unusual setting, it quickly turned into a real basketball environment as the sun set and night fell over the giant warship. 
 “Can we get a call, ref?” one sailor shouted. 
 “Get your head in the game!” said another. 
 The court was surrounded by stands of fans, so no loose balls had a chance of splashing in the water. 
 North Carolina won 67-55. 
 Obama’s stop in San Diego was the first leg of a nine-day diplomacy mission he’s embarking on, to Hawaii, where the U.S. is hosting an economic summit for the Asia-Pacific, and from there to Australia and Indonesia. He is aiming to use the trip to create bonds and boost U.S. business in the region before arriving back in Washington Nov. 20.
